steps.action: Set Ejection Seat Lever – DOWN & ARMED Set Harness Lever - FWD
steps.voiced: Set Ejection Seat Lever to DOWN and ARMED, Harness Lever to FWD.

steps.action: Parking Brake Lever – ENGAGED
steps.voiced: Set Parking Brake lever to ENGAGED.

steps.action: MASTER ARM switch – SAFE
steps.voiced: Set Master Arm switch to SAFE.

steps.action: Battery Switch – ON (Right Click) (Note: This will power the engine igniters, canopy) and IFEI (Integrated Fuel / Engine Indicator) panel.
steps.voiced: Set Battery Switch to ON.

steps.action: Check that hydraulic brake pressure gauge displays at least 3000 psi
steps.voiced: Confirm hydraulic brake pressure is at least 3000 psi.

steps.action: (Optional) Right click and hold Fire & Bleed Air Test switch to the "Channel A" (Forward) position to start the Fire/Bleed Air Test for Detection Loop Wait for the aural warnings sequence to finish before releasing the switch. The switch will be sprung back to the middle position on release. You should hear "Engine Fire Left, Engine Fire Left, Engine Fire Right, Engine Fire Right, APU Fire, APU Fire, Bleed Air Left, Bleed Air Left, Bleed Air Right, Bleed Air Right!" Left and right engine bleed air switches will be automatically closed during and after the test. We will have to re-open them later on.
steps.voiced: Optional — hold Fire and Bleed Air Test switch to Channel A and confirm all fire and bleed air warnings sound.

steps.action: (Optional) Cycle Battery switch to OFF (left click), then back to ON (right click) to rewind test audio tape. Alternatively, you can wait 10 seconds for the tape to rewind.
steps.voiced: Optional — cycle Battery switch OFF then ON to rewind the test audio tape.

steps.action: (Optional) Left click and hold Fire & Bleed Air Test switch to the "Channel B" (Aft) position to start the Fire/Bleed Air Test for Detection Loop B. Wait for the aural warnings sequence to finish before releasing the switch. The switch will be sprung back to the middle position. Parking brake shown in default ENGAGED position. The way to engage it is to rotate the lever in the vertical PARK position by left-clicking the lever, then pull it by scrolling the mousewheel. Ejection Seat Not Armed Ejection Seat Armed Fire & Bleed Air Test will illuminate all FIRE and BLEED cautions for the duration of the test.
steps.voiced: Optional — hold Fire and Bleed Air Test switch to Channel B and confirm all warnings sound.

steps.action: Verify that no APU ACC (Auxiliary Power Unit Accumulator) caution is visible on the Standby Caution Panel.
steps.voiced: Confirm no APU ACC caution on the Standby Caution Panel.

steps.action: Left click on the APU switch to set it to ON (FWD) to start the APU (Auxiliary Power Unit)
steps.voiced: Set APU switch to ON to start the Auxiliary Power Unit.

steps.action: Verify that the APU ACC caution is visible on the Standby Caution Panel and that the APU starts spooling up. In real life, the Plane Captain (PC) would give you this confirmation, but in DCS you can just listen for the APU spooling sound.
steps.voiced: Confirm APU ACC caution appears and APU spooling sound is heard.

steps.action: Once APU green light illuminates, the APU is now running. The APU will provides air pressure for the F404 engines' pneumatic ATS (Air Turbine Starter).
steps.voiced: Confirm APU green light illuminates — APU is running.

steps.action: Start right engine first by setting Engine Crank switch – R (RIGHT) using right-click. (Note: It's good practice to start the right engine first since it) provides most of the hydraulic pressure available for the brakes.
steps.voiced: Set Engine Crank switch to R to start the right engine first.

steps.action: Right Engine cranking will begin as the AMAD (Airframe Mounted Accessory Drive), which is pneumatically connected to the APU's starter, transmits power from the ATS to the engine. Engine RPM will rise to approx. 25 % RPM.
steps.voiced: Confirm right engine cranks and RPM rises to approximately 25%.

steps.action: When Right Engine RPM reaches 25 %, press RSHIFT+HOME to move the right throttle from the OFF detent to the IDLE detent to open the fuel valves and introduce fuel. Igniters will kick in and trigger an engine lightoff.
steps.voiced: At 25% RPM, move right throttle from OFF to IDLE — confirm engine lightoff.

steps.action: Verify that EGT (Exhaust Gas Temperature) does not exceed 750 deg C until engine stabilizes.
steps.voiced: Confirm EGT stays below 750 degrees C during stabilization.

steps.action: Wait for Right Engine RPM to stabilize around 60-65 % RPM. Confirm that right generator is running by checking that the R GEN caution is extinguished.
steps.voiced: Confirm right engine stabilizes at 60-65% RPM and R GEN caution clears.

steps.action: During engine start, the GPWS (Ground Proximity Warning System) and Flight Controls voice alert system will do a BIT (Built-In Test). Don't worry, that's normal. You will hear "Roll Left, Roll Left! Flight Controls, Flight Controls!" and a "Deedle deedle" sound.
steps.voiced: Confirm GPWS and Flight Controls BIT audio plays — this is normal during engine start.

steps.action: Reset MASTER CAUTION pushbutton by pressing it. Throttle to OFF Throttle to IDLE Engine Cranking Engine Running
steps.voiced: Press Master Caution pushbutton to reset it.

steps.action: Power Left and Right DDIs (Digital Display Indicator) by setting each DDI knob to DAY (right click).
steps.voiced: Set both DDI knobs to DAY to power up the displays.

steps.action: Turn on AMPCD (Advance Multi-Purpose Color Display) by setting its Brightness knob to the right
steps.voiced: Turn on the AMPCD by rotating its Brightness knob to the right.

steps.action: Turn HUD brightness control lever right to power Heads-Up Display.
steps.voiced: Turn HUD brightness control lever right to power up the HUD.

steps.action: Turn BLK LVL knob to the right
steps.voiced: Turn BLK LVL knob to the right.

steps.action: Set ALT switch to RDR to use the radar altimeter as an altitude reference
steps.voiced: Set ALT switch to RDR for radar altimeter reference.

steps.action: Set ATT switch to AUTO
steps.voiced: Set ATT switch to AUTO.

steps.action: Right-click Bleed Air Switch to rotate it 360 degrees clockwise from NORMAL back to NORMAL to re-open engine bleed air valves. You should hear the ECS (Environmental Control System) kick in as engine bleed air valves open.
steps.voiced: Rotate Bleed Air Switch 360 degrees clockwise to re-open bleed air valves — confirm ECS kicks in.

steps.action: Left click and hold CANOPY switch to CLOSE to close canopy.
steps.voiced: Hold CANOPY switch to CLOSE to close the canopy.

steps.action: Start INS (Inertial Navigation System) Alignment. INS GROUND ALIGNMENT PROCEDURE You can either use a Normal alignment (takes roughly 8 minutes) or a Stored Heading alignment (takes roughly 90 seconds), which results in a quicker alignment. If on ground, set INS Selector switch to GND (Ground) to start the INS alignment phase. If you wish to use a Stored Heading, from the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator) page, press the DDI button next to STD HDG, then the stored heading alignment will proceed and last for approx. 90 seconds. Otherwise, the normal alignment will proceed and last for 8 minutes. The alignment will take a few seconds to start. You can monitor the INS (Inertial Navigation System) Ground Alignment progress on the AMPCD. The GRND QUAL timer displays the time remaining in seconds. Once OK appears next to GRND QUAL, the INS alignment is complete. Set the INS selector to IFA (In-Flight Alignment) if a GPS is available (any mission set during 1986 or later should have it). POS/AINS mode (Position/Assisted-INS) will be visible on the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator), meaning that the GPS is used in conjunction with INS to provide the position of the aircraft. If no GPS is available (mission set before 1986), set INS selector to NAV instead. INS
steps.voiced: Set INS Selector to GND to begin alignment — use STD HDG for 90-second stored heading, or wait 8 minutes for normal alignment.

steps.action: Verify that there are no cautions for engine 2 (like HYD 2A, HYD 2B, R GEN)
steps.voiced: Confirm no HYD 2A, HYD 2B, or R GEN cautions for the right engine.

steps.action: Set left DDI (Digital Display Indicator) to the FCS (Flight Control System) page Press the OSB (Option Select Button) under TAC (Tactical) MENU to select the SUPT (Support) MENU Press the OSB next to FCS
steps.voiced: Set left DDI to the FCS page via TAC MENU then SUPT.

steps.action: Press the MASTER CAUTION pushbutton two times to re-stack the cautions and advisories together. The FCS page will then be more visible.
steps.voiced: Press Master Caution pushbutton twice to re-stack cautions.

steps.action: The "X"s on the FCS page indicate a FCS system error. In our case, the errors are probably due to wing droop caused by the aircraft being parked for too long.
steps.voiced: Confirm any X indications on FCS page — likely wing droop from parking.

steps.action: Press the FCS RESET button to reset FCS faults. "X"s should disappear.
steps.voiced: Press FCS RESET — confirm X indications clear.

steps.action: On the right DDI, the BIT FAILURES (Built-In Test) page is displayed. Press the OSB next to STOP to stop the indications from blinking. Cautions and advisories un-stacked Cautions and advisories re-stacked
steps.voiced: On the right DDI BIT FAILURES page, press OSB next to STOP.

steps.action: Hold the LIGHTS TEST switch to test lights. Make sure cautions illuminate properly, then release the switch.
steps.voiced: Hold LIGHTS TEST switch — confirm all caution lights illuminate, then release.

steps.action: Start left engine first by setting Engine Crank switch – L (LEFT) using left-click.
steps.voiced: Set Engine Crank switch to L to start the left engine.

steps.action: Left Engine cranking will begin as the AMAD (Airframe Mounted Accessory Drive), which is pneumatically connected to the APU's starter, transmits power from the ATS to the engine. Engine RPM will rise to approx. 25 % RPM.
steps.voiced: Confirm left engine cranks and RPM rises to approximately 25%.

steps.action: When Left Engine RPM reaches 25 %, press RALT+HOME to move the left throttle from the OFF detent to the IDLE detent to open the fuel valves and introduce fuel. Igniters will kick in and trigger an engine lightoff.
steps.voiced: At 25% RPM, move left throttle from OFF to IDLE — confirm engine lightoff.

steps.action: Verify that EGT (Exhaust Gas Temperature) does not exceed 750 deg C until engine stabilizes.
steps.voiced: Confirm EGT stays below 750 degrees C during stabilization.

steps.action: Wait for Left Engine RPM to stabilize around 60-65 % RPM. Confirm that left generator is running by checking that the L GEN caution is extinguished. Engine Cranking Engine Running Throttle to OFF Throttle to
steps.voiced: Confirm left engine stabilizes at 60-65% RPM and L GEN caution clears.

steps.action: Verify that there are no cautions for engine 1 (like HYD 1A, HYD 1B, L GEN)
steps.voiced: Confirm no HYD 1A, HYD 1B, or L GEN cautions for the left engine.

steps.action: Following the second engine start, the APU (Auxiliary Power Unit) will shutdown automatically approximately 1 minute after both engines are stabilized to IDLE.
steps.voiced: Confirm APU shuts down automatically about 1 minute after both engines stabilize.

steps.action: Set OBOGS (On-Board Oxygen Generating System) switch – ON
steps.voiced: Set OBOGS switch to ON.

steps.action: Set HOOK BYPASS switch to FIELD if operating on an airfield or to CARRIER if operating on an aircraft carrier.
steps.voiced: Set Hook Bypass to FIELD for airfield operations, CARRIER for carrier operations.

steps.action: Set FLAPS lever to HALF
steps.voiced: Set Flaps lever to HALF.

steps.action: Set ANTI-SKID switch ON if operating on a ground airfield Set ANTI-SKID switch OFF if operating on a carrier
steps.voiced: Set Anti-Skid ON for airfield operations, OFF for carrier operations.

steps.action: Set RADAR switch to OPERATE. While on the ground, radar operation is automatically inhibited.
steps.voiced: Set RADAR switch to OPERATE.

steps.action: Run FCS BIT Test On the BIT FAILURES page, click the OSB next to FCS- MC to enter the Flight Control System – Mission Computer Built-In Test page MC1 and MC2 status should be GO. FCSA and FCSB status should be PBIT GO. This means the FCS BIT Test needs to be performed. Press and hold "Y" key binding to hold the FCS BIT switch to ON (UP) position. While FCS BIT switch is held (Y), press the OSB next to "FCS" to start the FCS BIT test. FCSA and FCSB status will be "IN TEST" for the duration of the test.
steps.voiced: On BIT FAILURES page, press FCS-MC, then hold FCS BIT switch and press OSB next to FCS to start BIT test.

steps.action: Flight controls will move for the duration of the test. Test will be complete when the FCSA and FCSB status will be GO.
steps.voiced: Confirm flight controls move during test — wait for FCSA and FCSB to show GO.

steps.action: Adjust AMPCD Gain, Contrast and Symbology brightness as required. This will greatly improve readability. For night operations, I would recommend setting AMPCD mode to NGT since the brightness of the moving map can easily blind you in low light conditions.
steps.voiced: Adjust AMPCD gain, contrast, and brightness — use NGT mode for night operations.

steps.action: Set ZULU time in the cockpit. On the AMPCD, press the OSB next to the TIMEUFC.
steps.voiced: On the AMPCD, press the OSB next to TIMEUFC to set ZULU time.

steps.action: Then, go on the UFC (Up-Front Controller) and press the OSB next to ZTOD to display ZULU time on the Heads-Up Display once the aircraft wheels are OFF the ground.
steps.voiced: On the UFC, press the OSB next to ZTOD to display ZULU time on HUD after liftoff.

steps.action: Press the T/O TRIM button to set the aircraft trim for takeoff configuration.
steps.voiced: Press the T/O TRIM button to set takeoff trim.

steps.action: Set PITOT HEAT switch – AUTO (only use ON in case of icing conditions on ground since AUTO inhibits Pitot Heat on ground to maximize available power on takeoff)
steps.voiced: Set PITOT HEAT switch to AUTO.

steps.action: Set HUD repeater on the left DDI Press the OSB next to MENU to enter the TAC page Press the OSB next to HUD
steps.voiced: Set HUD repeater on the left DDI via TAC MENU then HUD.

steps.action: Set FCS page on right DDI Press the OSB next to MENU twice to enter the FCS page Press the OSB next to FCS
steps.voiced: Set FCS page on right DDI via MENU twice then FCS.

steps.action: If you are on the ground, verify that WINGS FOLD lever is in the SPREAD position (they may be folded if you start from an aircraft carrier). If wings are folded, right click on the lever to set it to SPREAD, wait for the wings to deploy all the way and confirm visually that the wings are deployed properly. Then, scroll mousewheel to push the WING FOLD lever IN (PUSHED). The WING UNLK caution should disappear on the HUD Repeater once wings are spread and locked. Keep your wings folded if on an aircraft carrier.
steps.voiced: Confirm wings spread and locked for shore ops — keep folded if on a carrier.

steps.action: Set Radar Altimeter warning index to 200 ft if you intend to takeoff from a ground airfield (shore) or 80 ft from a carrier.
steps.voiced: Set radar altimeter warning index to 200 ft for shore, 80 ft for carrier.

steps.action: Uncage Standby Attitude Indicator. Red flag should be removed.
steps.voiced: Uncage Standby Attitude Indicator — confirm red flag is removed.

steps.action: Set BINGO FUEL (minimum fuel needed to return to base) based on your mission profile by using the UP or DOWN arrow buttons on the IFEI panel. Usually I set 8000 lbs.
steps.voiced: Set BINGO FUEL on the IFEI panel per your mission profile.

steps.action: Press the ALR-67 PWR button to power the Radar Warning Receiver (RWR)
steps.voiced: Press ALR-67 PWR button to power the Radar Warning Receiver.

steps.action: Set ECM selector to STBY.
steps.voiced: Set ECM selector to STBY.

steps.action: Set DISPENSER switch to ON (Middle).
steps.voiced: Set DISPENSER switch to ON.

steps.action: Power Up IFF (Identify-Friend-or-Foe) system by pressing the IFF Button, then holding the ON button on the UFC for a few seconds. When « XP » is displayed, the IFF has been powered up correctly. Verify that Mode 4A is selected (« : » means selected). If 4A is not selected, press OSB (Option Select Button) next to « 4A ».
steps.voiced: Press IFF on UFC, hold ON until XP displays — confirm Mode 4A is selected.

steps.action: Power Up Link-16 Datalink system by pressing the D/L Button a first time to display the Link-4 datalink options, then press the D/L Button a second time to display the Link-16 datalink options. Then, hold the ON button on the UFC for a few seconds. When « ON » is displayed, the Link-16 Datalink system has been powered up correctly.
steps.voiced: Press D/L twice to access Link-16 options, then hold ON until ON displays.

steps.action: On UFC, set Link 16 desired Datalink Frequency by entering it on the scratchpad, then pressing « ENT ». 127 is used by default, you can keep it.
steps.voiced: Enter the Link 16 datalink frequency on the UFC scratchpad, then press ENT.

steps.action: Adjust all volume knobs on the Audio Panel as required. Take special care to check that the MIDS A and MIDS B volume knobs are turned up (Multifunctional Information Distribution System).
steps.voiced: Adjust Audio Panel volume knobs — confirm MIDS A and MIDS B are turned up.

steps.action: Verify on MIDS SUPT page that Datalink information is correct (Network, AIC, F/F, Voice frequencies).
steps.voiced: Confirm MIDS SUPT page shows correct datalink network, AIC, F/F, and voice frequencies.

steps.action: Equip either JHMCS (Joint Helmet-Mounted Cueing System) helmet or NVGs (Night Vision Goggles) as required. Press "\" (communication menu binding) to contact ground crew Press "F8" to select "Ground Crew" Press "F8" to "Change helmet-mounted device". Press either "F1" for JHMCS or "F2" for NVGs.
steps.voiced: Equip JHMCS or NVGs as required via Ground Crew menu.

steps.action: Adjust Seat Height as desired. (Note: steps 71 to 76 are only required if using the JHMCS (Joint Helmet-) Mounted Cueing System).
steps.voiced: Adjust seat height as desired — steps 71-76 apply only if using JHMCS.

steps.action: [JHMCS] Turn HMD brightness knob to BRT (Bright).
steps.voiced: Set HMD brightness knob to BRT.

steps.action: [JHMCS] Perform HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display) BIT (Built-In Test). Go in the SUPT (Support) page. Press OSB next to BIT (Built-In-Test). Press OSB next to DISPLAYS (NOT RDY). Press on OSB next to HMD to start Helmet-Mounted Display Built-In Test. "PBIT GO" indicates that the test is ready to be run.
steps.voiced: On SUPT page, press BIT then DISPLAYS then HMD to start the HMD Built-In Test.

steps.action: [JHMCS] Wait until the HMD test is finished. The IN TEST indication will first blink on your helmet. While test symbology cycles on your helmet display, the BIT page will indicate "HMD IN TEST". When test symbology starts showing the same pictures, the test can be considered complete.
steps.voiced: Wait for HMD test to complete — BIT page shows HMD IN TEST during test.

steps.action: [JHMCS] Stop the HMD test by pressing the OSB next to "STOP". The BIT page will then indicate "HMD GO".
steps.voiced: Press OSB next to STOP to end HMD test — confirm HMD GO.

steps.action: [JHMCS] Perform Coarse Helmet Display Alignment. Go in the SUPT (Support) page. Press OSB next to HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display). Press OSB next to ALIGN to start JHMCS alignment. "READY" indication will then appear on your helmet cross. Move helmet cross on the alignment reference cross of the Heads- Up Display (HUD). When helmet cross and HUD cross are roughly aligned, press and hold the Cage/Uncage Button ("C" binding) for more than 2.5 sec while keeping both crosses aligned. While the alignment process is taking place, the "ALIGNING" indication is visible. The "ALIGN OK" indication appears when the alignment is complete. You can then release the Cage/Uncage button.
steps.voiced: On SUPT page press HMD then ALIGN — align helmet cross with HUD cross, then hold Cage/Uncage until ALIGN OK.

steps.action: [JHMCS] If required, perform Fine Helmet Display Alignment. The coarse alignment may not be perfect and may require a fine adjustment (FA). The "FA DX/DY" indication means that an adjustment is needed for the horizontal and vertical axis. (Throttle Designator Controller) AFT/FWD/LEFT/RIGHT controls to align the upper small cross with the HUD reference cross. Press the Cage/Uncage button to select the roll fine adjustment (FA ROLL). (Throttle Designator Controller) LEFT/RIGHT controls to align the lower small cross with the vertical line of the HUD reference cross. When fine alignment is satisfactory, press the OSB next to ALIGN to unbox it. This will save the alignment.
steps.voiced: If FA DX/DY shows, use TDC to align upper and lower crosses with HUD reference, then press ALIGN to save.

steps.action: Load the Data Transfer Cartridge (DTC) data via the MUMI (Memory Unit Mission Initialization) page. The DTC contains data for countermeasure programs, communication channels, navigation waypoints, targets, threats, datalink settings, route points, weapon settings, and more. Verify that DTC is set up and inserted. Go in the SUPT (Support) page. Press OSB next to MUMI (Memory Unit Mission Initialization). Press OSB (Option Select Button) next to each data type. This will load individual DTC partitions. (Note: As of 17/04/2025, only ALR67 and COMM data types can be loaded. More data types) will become available later on as DTC settings are integrated into DCS World. DTC items will become boxed as their respective data type is loaded.
steps.voiced: On SUPT page, press MUMI and press each data type OSB to load DTC partitions.

steps.action: Set STROBE switch ON
steps.voiced: Set STROBE switch to ON.

steps.action: Set POSITION LIGHTS switch to BRT
steps.voiced: Set POSITION LIGHTS switch to BRT.

steps.action: Set FORMATION LIGHTS to BRT
steps.voiced: Set FORMATION LIGHTS to BRT.

steps.action: Turn on lights using the throttle Exterior Lights switch ("L") as required.
steps.voiced: Turn on exterior lights as required.

steps.action: Set LANDING/TAXI light switch – ON (UP)
steps.voiced: Set LANDING/TAXI light switch to ON.

steps.action: Left click on Parking Brake lever to disengage it (PUSHED = DISENGAGED).
steps.voiced: Disengage Parking Brake lever.

steps.action: Remove chocks if installed (this is usually the case when operating on an aircraft carrier) Make sure your canopy is open to communicate to the ground crew Press "\" (communication menu binding) to contact ground crew Press "F8" to select "Ground Crew" Press "F4" to select "Wheel Chocks" Press "F2" to "Remove Wheel Chocks". Canopy Open
steps.voiced: Remove wheel chocks via Ground Crew menu.
